notes.note: Marcus Tullius Tiro: Cicero's slave, manumitted around 53 BC, secretary, literary collaborator, and lifelong intimate. The recipient of all letters in Ad Familiares book 16.
Si me diligis: a recurring formula in the Tiro letters --- the household imperative, much warmer than its surface and used for things that matter most.

notes.anchor: fourth day before the Ides of April
notes.note: 10 April. The Ides of April fall on the 13th; Romans counted backwards inclusively from the named day.
